violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the inside bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Indoor bar will use 3-bay sink until bar machine is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2025

ROP imitation crab was observed thawing with package in tact. Proper thawing procedures reviewed with PIC. Imitation crab was disposed of. Food was observed at 41 degrees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2025

Soap, container lid, and a container of spice were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024
The hand-washing sink in the bar was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

Raw animal foods (such as chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as lettuce) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Dishwasher was taken out of service and must not be used until it is repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024

Turkey, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Turkey was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Per person in charge, turkey was cooked the day prior. Turkey was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Proper cooling methods were reviewed with the person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Large pieces of meat (turkey) were placed in the refrigerator. Proper cooling methods were reviewed with the person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by flies in the kitchen and small bar. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment must schedule pest control services. Please email proof of services. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024
The refrigerator door gaskets next to the freezer in the kitchen were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024

Vacuum packaged chicken, in the refrigerator, is from an unapproved source. Per person in charge, a club member brought in the chicken for an event. Food must be obtained from an approved source. Chicken was embargoed during time of inspection and must not be used until the embargo is released by inspector. Embargo stickers were placed on the chicken. Embargo sticker seal must not be broken. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2023

Alcohol was observed stored at the handwashing sink at the bar. Alcohol must not be stored at the handwashing sink to prevent contamination by employees washing their hands. New Violation. Correct By: 07/05/2023

Ice is stored in the ice bin adjacent to the handwashing sink where it is subject to splash. Food (ice) must be stored where it is not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination. Per person in charge, a splash guard will be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2023

Knives are stored on an unclean surface (between handwashing sink and ice bin at the bar) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Knives were removed during time of inspection and must not be stored between the handwashing sink and ice bin. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2023

Linens (clean wiping rags) were observed stored on the handwashing sink at the bar. Lines must not be stored at the handwashing sink to prevent contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2023

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar at both ice wells. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2023

Wood is used in the construction of shelves, walls and the knife rack in the kitchen closet areas, and kitchen cabinets. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2023
The ceiling above the walk-in cooler area is in poor repair. Ceiling tiles are missing. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2023
